_comment: TEMPLATE ONLY. The real file must NOT live in this public repo — it belongs only in the private repo the Worker reads from (see access-control/README.md). Every condition's 'field' must be one of the canonical whoami fields loader.js sends: username, email, country, insertSite, langcode, displayName (plus any custom field already in use, subject to the acting admin's own bucket's allowedFields checklist — see PERMISSIONS_GUIDE.md). 'grants' arrays replace the old single 'tier' string — a user can hold more than one at once (e.g. ["user","dev","beta_0"]). 'beta_0' is a wildcard meaning 'all betas'; any other 'beta_N' gates one specific feature. Omitting 'grants' defaults to ["user"]. 'bucketId' on every entry is admin-layer metadata only (which delegated admin group owns this entry) — the live access check never reads it; omit it (or use null) for anything root-owned. 'override'/'blacklist'/'allow'/'extraGrants' are ALL the same shape now — {id, bucketId, conditions, grants?} — AND within one entry's conditions[], OR across entries in the same section; 'id' just needs to be unique per section (the admin tool always generates a real one). A missing/empty conditions[] never matches anyone — evalGroup() explicitly guards against that being a vacuous wildcard. Special grants (dev/beta_*) on override/allow/extraGrants are root-only in the admin tool; override/extraGrants themselves are root-only sections entirely.
